meta.description: A named projection of a subtree into document form — maps tree depth and item type to document roles (heading, body, caption, etc.) so any tree can be exported or rendered as a well-formed document without restructuring the data. Per the flat one-level rule the two nested maps are children: expandState.exceptions → `document-expand-exception` children ({itemId, depth}, depth −1 = collapse), and roleMap.byDepth / roleMap.byType → `document-role-by-depth` / `document-role-by-type` children. expandState.defaultDepth is flattened onto this payload as `defaultDepth`.

targetId.description: UUID of the item whose subtree this document projects.

name.description: Human-readable name for this document (e.g. 'Executive summary', 'Onboarding guide').

mode.description: How the projected subtree is rendered. 'document' (default) = a well-formed document per roleMap; 'tree' = the interactive tree; 'todo' = the tree with a completion checkbox on each item bound to its completedAt field (checking sets completedAt to now, unchecking clears it).

defaultDepth.description: Number of levels below targetId to expand by default (flattened from expandState.defaultDepth). Per-item overrides are `document-expand-exception` children.

isOrgDefault.description: When true, this is the canonical org-level document for targetId. At most one document per targetId should have this set.

baseDocumentId.description: UUID of the document this was forked from (set on fork-on-modify). Null for original documents.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
